message:
feat(fwc): atomic write-then-rename for credential, config, lock, and preference files

Replace direct fs::write() calls with a temp-file → sync_all → rename
pattern across all four fwc persistence paths:

- credential.rs (FileBackend::put): Credential JSON files now written
  to .tmp suffix, synced, then atomically renamed. On Unix, file mode
  0o600 is set via OpenOptions::mode() at creation time rather than
  post-creation chmod. Temp file cleaned up on error.

- main.rs (save_context_config): Host context TOML files use the same
  atomic write pattern. Error message updated to "failed to safely
  write host context file".

- op_lock.rs (LockStore::persist): Lock store JSON files gain crash
  safety via atomic rename. Error message updated accordingly.

- routing.rs (PreferenceStore::save): Routing preference JSON files
  follow the same pattern with temp-file cleanup on failure.

All four paths share the same structure: create temp file with .tmp
suffix → write full content → sync_all() → rename over target →
clean up temp on error. On Windows, the existing file is removed
before rename since Windows does not support atomic rename-over.

This prevents partial/corrupt files when the process is killed mid-write,
which is common in multi-agent environments where connectors may be
restarted at any time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
